Case title: AI-assisted memoing of ethnographic or historical fieldnotes
Short description: Compare memo trails and final claims produced from the same fieldnote corpus with and without AI assistance.
Empirical unit of analysis: Fieldnotes, memo histories, and final analytic claims
Key typology question: Does later interpretive correction rely on a role distinct from the interviewer who produced the interaction?
Observable artifacts / traces: Memo versions, ambiguity-retention markers, analytic corrections, reflexive logs
Key outcomes observed: Category stabilization, ambiguity retained versus collapsed, claim revisions
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: AI-assisted and human-only memo trails converge without loss of contextual subtlety
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Some fieldnotes are highly systematic and easier for AI than live field settings
Why this case has high leverage: It is the best case for making tacit contextual knowledge visible after data collection is over.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
